item1_businessGoal.description: Business goal is clear and tied to delivery-flow improvement
evidence: Story states: 'Delivery managers and integrating clients need a stable, human-meaningful identifier to reference individual work items in external tools, dashboards, and reports.'
Problem statement explains friction caused by absence of project-scoped issue number in current API responses.
User story follows canonical Given/When/Then structure tied to delivery visibility.

item2_acceptanceCriteria.description: Acceptance criteria are testable and unambiguous
evidence: 8 acceptance criteria, each with explicit Given/When/Then structure.
AC3 precisely requires field named 'issueId'; forbids 'iid' in response — directly verifiable.
AC4 ties the value to a known GitLab iid=N, making the assertion deterministic.
AC5 names the exact script (scripts/smoke-test.sh) and both endpoints.
AC7 names the exact document (documentation/context-map.md) and required content.
No ambiguous criteria found.

item3_lockedConstraints.description: Locked constraints are present and match the original requirement
evidence: Story § 'Locked Request Constraints' enumerates 7 items covering: field naming, both endpoints, source-of-truth mapping, additive-only scope, documentation update, test update, and smoke-test update.
Checkpoint requirementLock (9 items) is a superset — two additional items (Issue model carriage, GitLabIssueResponse/mapper mapping) are implementation details correctly deferred to the plan.
All checkpoint non-negotiable constraints are present verbatim or in equivalent form in the story.
Story § 'In Scope': 6 explicit items covering both endpoints, mapping source, test updates, smoke-test, and context-map update.
Story § 'Out of Scope': 5 explicit items covering renaming/removing fields, other endpoints, global id, filtering/sorting, and auth/pagination changes.
Boundaries align with additive-only contract constraint.

item5_openQuestions.description: No unresolved open questions that would block implementation
evidence: Story § 'Open Questions': 'None.'
Story § 'Locked Request Constraints': 'Unresolved items: None.'
Checkpoint openBlockers: []

item6_planSections.description: All required sections present in the plan
evidence: Requirement Lock / Source Of Truth: present — includes original request source, non-negotiable constraints, change-scope constraints, documentation constraints, test constraints, and assumptions.
Payload Examples: present — before/after for both POST /api/issues/search and POST /api/issues.
Validation Boundary Decision: present — 3-row table with rule, boundary, and rationale.
Class Structure: present — 16-row table plus field-position convention with Java snippets.
Implementation Slices: present — 6 ordered slices with files changed, unit/integration/component tests, and edge-case coverage.
Testing Matrix: present — 7-row table covering unit (5 classes), integration (1 class), component (1 class).
Logging Requirements: present — exact before/after log.debug() call specified.
Documentation Update Requirements: present — 4-row table covering context-map.md, issues.http, smoke-test.sh, and plan itself.

item7_requirementLockMatch.description: Requirement lock in plan matches requirement lock in story
evidence: Plan § 'Requirement Lock': 'Client-facing field name MUST be issueId; iid must NEVER appear in any API response payload' matches story constraint.
Plan: 'Both POST /api/issues/search and POST /api/issues responses must include issueId' matches story constraint.
Plan: 'issueId maps directly and only from GitLab iid' matches story constraint.
Plan: 'Additive only — no existing field is renamed, removed, or retyped' matches story constraint.
Plan: 'No new endpoints, configuration keys, or architecture boundaries introduced' matches story constraint.
Plan: 'documentation/context-map.md updated' and 'scripts/smoke-test.sh updated' match story constraints.
Plan: 'All affected unit, integration, and component tests updated' matches story constraint.
No contradictions or omissions found between plan and story requirement locks.

item8_payloadExamples.description: Payload examples show before/after for both endpoints with issueId additive
evidence: POST /api/issues/search Before: 8-field issue object without issueId.
POST /api/issues/search After: same 8 fields plus issueId:7 inserted as 2nd field — additive only.
POST /api/issues Before: 9-field object without issueId.
POST /api/issues After: same 9 fields plus issueId:12 inserted as 2nd field — additive only.
issueId values (7, 12) are intentionally distinct from id values (123456, 700) to catch field-confusion bugs.

item9_classStructure.description: Class structure is accurate and complete (covers all 5 required layers)
evidence: GitLabIssueResponse: listed — add long iid as 2nd record component after id.
Issue: listed — add long issueId as 2nd record component after id.
IssueDto: listed — add long issueId as 2nd record component after id.
GitLabIssuesMapper: listed — pass issueResponse.iid() as issueId in toIssue(); update debug log.
IssuesResponseMapper: listed — pass issue.issueId() as issueId in toIssueDto().
All 5 required classes covered; additionally 11 test files and 3 non-Java artifacts (stub, smoke-test, docs) are included.
Java record snippets show field position convention consistently across all 3 records.
Layer boundary honored: iid stays in integration layer; issueId used in orchestration and REST layers.

item10_slicesOrdering.description: Implementation slices are independently executable and ordered correctly
evidence: Slice 1: Add iid to GitLabIssueResponse + update WireMock stub — foundation, no dependencies.
Slice 2: Add issueId to Issue + map iid→issueId in GitLabIssuesMapper — correctly depends on Slice 1.
Slice 3: Add issueId to IssueDto + map in IssuesResponseMapper — correctly depends on Slice 2.
Slice 4: Update IssuesControllerIT — correctly deferred until production code is complete (Slices 1-3).
Slice 5: Update IssuesApiComponentTest — correctly deferred; relies on stub update from Slice 1.
Slice 6: Smoke test + docs + HTTP examples — independent of production code slices.
Each slice lists specific files changed and verify-quick.sh gate, making them independently executable.
No circular dependencies found.

item11_testingMatrix.description: Testing matrix covers unit, integration, and component levels with named classes
evidence: Unit (5 named classes): GitLabIssuesMapperTest, IssuesServiceTest, IssuePageTest, IssuesResponseMapperTest, SearchIssuesResponseTest.
Integration (1 named class): IssuesControllerIT — Spring @WebMvcTest slice proving JSON serialization.
Component (1 named class): IssuesApiComponentTest — WireMock-backed end-to-end through deserialization.
Coverage notes explain what each level proves that others cannot (e.g., component validates Jackson deserialization of iid from GitLab stub).
No level duplicates another level's scenarios.

item12_smokeTestSpec.description: Smoke test update is explicitly specified with exact assertions
evidence: Slice 6 provides complete bash for check_field helper function with correct logic (curl + grep for field presence).
Two exact check_field invocations specified: POST /api/issues/search and POST /api/issues both asserting 'issueId' field present.
Placement specified: after existing check calls.
Variable SMOKE_FIELD_TITLE pattern matches existing smoke-test title generation style.
AC5 traceability row confirms smoke-test assertion verifies AC5.

item13_issueIdNaming.description: issueId field naming enforced throughout (never iid in client-facing output)
evidence: IssueDto record component named issueId — Jackson serializes record component names as-is, no @JsonProperty needed.
Issue orchestration model uses issueId — iid never escapes integration layer.
GitLabIssueResponse.iid() is integration-layer only; plan Requirement Lock: 'GitLabIssueResponse is package-private to the integration layer'.
Validation Boundary Decision row 3 explicitly states: 'issueId must not leak as iid in responses'.
jsonPath assertions in IssuesControllerIT test $.issueId not $.iid.
IssuesApiComponentTest asserts path('issueId') not path('iid').

item14_noNewBoundaries.description: No new endpoints, config keys, or architecture boundaries introduced
evidence: Plan Requirement Lock explicitly states: 'No new endpoints, configuration keys, or architecture boundaries introduced.'
Class Structure table shows all changes are Modify (no new classes or interfaces).
Out of Scope section lists: 'Adding issueId to any endpoint other than the two issue endpoints listed above.'
Constitution Principle 2 (Ports and Adapters) compliance maintained — integration adapter still maps to orchestration contract; no boundary crossed.
No new Spring beans, configuration properties, or Feign client methods required.

Documentation Update Requirements table row 1: 'documentation/context-map.md — Add issueId to the IssueDto field description; add note that issueId maps from GitLab iid. Update test count if affected.'

Slice 6 provides exact replacement text: 'IssueDto is the single API response shape for all issue endpoints; fields: id (GitLab global id), issueId (project-scoped number, maps from GitLab iid), title, description, state, labels, assignee, milestone, parent'
AC7 traceability row confirms this update verifies AC7.

phase1Decision.reason: All 15 Phase 1 checks pass. Story has clear business goal, 8 testable and unambiguous ACs, fully enumerated locked constraints, explicit scope boundaries, and no open questions. Plan contains all required sections; requirement lock precisely mirrors story constraints; payload examples are additive and correct; all 5 required class layers are covered with accurate field-position conventions; 6 slices are correctly ordered with no circular dependencies; testing matrix names 7 classes across all 3 test levels; smoke-test update is specified with exact bash; issueId naming is enforced at every layer boundary; no new endpoints or architecture boundaries are introduced; context-map.md update is explicitly specified in Slice 6.

evidence: All 16 changed files listed in the brief are present in changedFiles in the implementation report.
Report slicesCompleted: [1,2,3,4,5,6] — all 6 plan slices completed.
All 5 production classes changed (GitLabIssueResponse, Issue, IssueDto, GitLabIssuesMapper, IssuesResponseMapper).
All 7 test classes updated plus WireMock stub, smoke-test.sh, context-map.md, issues.http.
Report deviationsFromPlan: [] — no deviations.
GitLabIssueResponse.java: 'long iid' is the 2nd record component (after 'id'); no @JsonProperty — field name matches GitLab JSON name exactly.
Issue.java: 'long issueId' is 2nd record component after 'id'; iid does not appear in this file.
IssueDto.java: 'long issueId' is 2nd record component after 'id'; iid does not appear in this file.
GitLabIssuesMapper.java: issueResponse.iid() passed as issueId to Issue constructor; debug log includes 'issueId={}'.
IssuesResponseMapper.java: issue.issueId() passed as issueId to IssueDto constructor.
iid is confined to integration layer (GitLabIssueResponse + GitLabIssuesMapper); issueId is used in orchestration (Issue) and REST (IssueDto) layers.
No new architecture boundaries, endpoints, or config keys introduced.
Record component ordering: long iid is 2nd in GitLabIssueResponse; long issueId is 2nd in Issue and IssueDto — consistent with plan field-position convention.
Nullability: issueId declared as primitive long — no nullability issue possible.
Logging: debug log in GitLabIssuesMapper includes 'issueId={}' as 2nd positional arg after 'id={}'; format matches existing log pattern.
No @JsonProperty needed — 'issueId' Java field name matches the desired JSON key directly (Jackson serializes record components by name).

phase2Decision.reason: All 11 Phase 2 checks are PASS or N/A with justification. Implementation matches all 6 plan slices with 16 changed files and zero deviations. Architecture boundaries are correctly enforced: iid confined to integration layer, issueId used in orchestration and REST layers. All 7 test classes updated with named assertions at unit, integration, and component levels. Reviewer-independent rerun of scripts/final-check.sh confirms BUILD SUCCESS with Spotless, Checkstyle, PMD, CPD, SpotBugs (0 bugs), and JaCoCo all passing. Documentation (context-map.md, issues.http) and smoke-test.sh are updated correctly.
